full_text:
# Tribus documentation

- it is on GitHub\'s main branch in the docs folder

- using Sphinx language to create it

 - [[https://docs.readthedocs.io/en/stable/intro/getting-started-with-sphinx.html]{.underline}](https://docs.readthedocs.io/en/stable/intro/getting-started-with-sphinx.html)

 - [[https://www.sphinx-doc.org/en/master/usage/quickstart.html]{.underline}](https://www.sphinx-doc.org/en/master/usage/quickstart.html)

- The basic file is 'index.rst' which defines the basic structure of
 > the documentation and also defines which other files will be used

- There is a separate tutorial in the 'tutorial.rst''

- the documentation of all the functions is in 'tribus.rst' (It's
 > called API Reference in the documentation, since this is the
 > standard naming)

- You can add code blocks (there is an example already in the
 > documentation)

 - if you want to display the output of the codeblock (for example
 > a table or a figure), you have to add it manually as inserting
 > a figure or a table in the document (examples in the docs)

- There is a specific nice way to describe the functions with all the
 > parameters (there are already examples in the code)

- You can build the html files by the command in the terminal: make
 > html

 - the html will be created in the build folder, you can open the
 > index.html

xamples in the code)

- You can build the html files by the command in the terminal: make
 > html

 - the html will be created in the build folder, you can open the
 > index.html

- All the figures, csv-s that you want to display should be stored in
 > the 'source' folder
